description: Adopt fast-check property-based testing across the three TypeScript surfaces in the harness (parseCronFile/loadCrons, path-guard sensitive-path matching, oh CLI flag detection). Property tests serve as regression prevention; source review confirmed all target invariants already hold. Two small refactors expose currently module-private functions for testing. All stories execute via /delegate (sandbox-side workers); orchestrator only scaffolds and reviews.

userStories.title: Install fast-check and extend vitest include glob
userStories.description: As a sandbox worker, I add fast-check to root devDependencies (and apps/oh devDependencies if root hoisting does not satisfy apps/oh typecheck) and extend vitest.config.ts to cover the apps/oh test directory, so all subsequent property tests run under the existing npm test script with no additional CI configuration.
acceptanceCriteria: fast-check added to devDependencies in /home/sandbox/harness/package.json (pin to current stable ^3.x — confirm with `npm view fast-check version`)
If npm test from repo root cannot typecheck apps/oh/src/__tests__/cli.property.test.ts (added in US-008) due to module resolution, also add fast-check to apps/oh/package.json devDependencies
package-lock.json (or pnpm-lock.yaml) updated by running the appropriate install command
vitest.config.ts include glob extended to add 'apps/**/__tests__/**/*.test.ts' (existing entries for scripts/__tests__/ and .pi/**/__tests__/ unchanged)
Paste `npx vitest list` output in the PR description confirming all property test files (US-002 through US-008) are discovered by vitest
`npm test` (or `pnpm test`) from repo root completes successfully with no new failures
No changes to existing example tests
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Never-throw regression-prevention property for parseCronFile
userStories.description: As a sandbox worker, I add a property test asserting parseCronFile(content, filePath) returns either null or a valid CronEntry but never throws, for any arbitrary string inputs. Current implementation already satisfies this; test is regression prevention against future YAML-parser-library adoption.
acceptanceCriteria: New file scripts/__tests__/cron-runtime.property.test.ts exists
Contains: `fc.assert(fc.property(fc.string(), fc.string(), (content, filePath) => { expect(() => parseCronFile(content, filePath)).not.toThrow(); }))`
Uses fast-check default numRuns (100); no tuning
No modifications to scripts/cron-runtime.ts source (FR-11)
`npm test` passes
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Forward-compatibility regression-prevention property for parseCronFile
userStories.description: As a sandbox worker, I add a property test asserting that valid cron frontmatter remains parseable when arbitrary unknown YAML keys are appended. Current implementation already ignores unknown keys (parser only reads named keys); test is regression prevention.
acceptanceCriteria: In same file scripts/__tests__/cron-runtime.property.test.ts, add a second property test
Constructs baseline valid frontmatter with `schedule: "* * * * *"` and body
Appends 1–5 arbitrary `key: value` pairs using constrained arbitraries: keys = `fc.stringMatching(/^[a-zA-Z_][a-zA-Z0-9_]*$/)`, values = `fc.stringMatching(/^[^\n:#]+$/)`
Calls parseCronFile(extendedContent, 'test.md') and asserts result is non-null and result.schedule === '* * * * *'
No modifications to scripts/cron-runtime.ts source (FR-11; verified at scripts/cron-runtime.ts:27-33)
`npm test` passes
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Ordering-stability regression-prevention property for loadCrons
userStories.description: As a sandbox worker, I add a property test asserting loadCrons(dir) returns entries in deterministic alphabetical order by filename EVEN WHEN files are written in non-alphabetical order. loadCrons already sorts (scripts/cron-runtime.ts:50); test prevents accidental removal in future refactoring. Critical: the reverse-write fixture is what actually exercises the sort invariant.
acceptanceCriteria: In scripts/__tests__/cron-runtime.property.test.ts, add a third property test
Generates 2–8 unique filenames using `fc.uniqueArray(fc.stringMatching(/^[a-z]{1,8}\.md$/), { minLength: 2, maxLength: 8 })`
Creates temp directory with `fs.mkdtempSync(path.join(os.tmpdir(), 'cron-prop-'))`

WRITES files in REVERSE-alphabetical order (sort filenames descending) with minimal valid frontmatter `---
schedule: "* * * * *"
---
`
Calls loadCrons(tmpDir), extracts returned entries' filePath values
Asserts returned order is ASCENDING alphabetical sort of input filenames
Cleans up with `fs.rmSync(tmpDir, { recursive: true, force: true })` inside try/finally
No modifications to scripts/cron-runtime.ts source (FR-11)
`npm test` passes
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Refactor path-guard.ts to expose isSensitivePath and SENSITIVE_PATHS as named exports
userStories.description: As a sandbox worker, I refactor .pi/extensions/path-guard.ts to extract the sensitive-path check into a named exported pure function isSensitivePath(path: string): boolean, and export the SENSITIVE_PATHS regex list as a named constant, so US-006 can test against the same compiled regexes without duplication-drift risk. Existing default-export extension function unchanged in behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: `.pi/extensions/path-guard.ts` adds `export` to existing `const SENSITIVE_PATHS: RegExp[] = [ ... ];` declaration at line 3
`.pi/extensions/path-guard.ts` exports `export function isSensitivePath(path: string): boolean { return SENSITIVE_PATHS.some((re) => re.test(path)); }`
Default export's internal sensitive-path check at line 55 is replaced with `isSensitivePath(path)` call
Existing .pi/extensions/__tests__/path-guard.test.ts example tests continue to pass with no modifications
path-guard.ts is NOT on .claude/protected-paths.txt (verified) — no override note required
`npm test` passes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Completed 2026-05-24; commit ccff7a1. Wave 5. Added export to SENSITIVE_PATHS, inserted isSensitivePath named export after array, replaced inline .some() check with isSensitivePath call at line 59. All 117 tests pass (32 path-guard).

userStories.title: No-false-positives property for isSensitivePath
userStories.description: As a sandbox worker, I add a property test asserting isSensitivePath(path) returns false for any arbitrary path string that does not match any sensitive-path regex.
acceptanceCriteria: New file .pi/extensions/__tests__/path-guard.property.test.ts exists
Imports `isSensitivePath` AND `SENSITIVE_PATHS` (both added in US-005) directly from `.pi/extensions/path-guard` — no re-declaration
Property: generates arbitrary path strings using regex non-match filter (NOT substring exclusion): `fc.string().filter((s) => !SENSITIVE_PATHS.some((re) => re.test(s)))`
Asserts `isSensitivePath(s) === false` for every such generated path
Uses fast-check default numRuns (100)
`npm test` passes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Completed 2026-05-24. Created .pi/extensions/__tests__/path-guard.property.test.ts with regex non-match filter (fc.string().filter(...)) and isSensitivePath(s) === false assertion. Imports both isSensitivePath and SENSITIVE_PATHS from ../path-guard. pnpm test passes (118 tests, 11 files).

userStories.title: Add export to isHelpFlag and isVersionFlag in apps/oh/src/cli.ts
userStories.description: As a sandbox worker, I add the `export` keyword to isHelpFlag and isVersionFlag in apps/oh/src/cli.ts so they can be imported from a property test file. No behavior changes.
acceptanceCriteria: apps/oh/src/cli.ts line 19: `function isHelpFlag(...)` → `export function isHelpFlag(...)`
apps/oh/src/cli.ts line 23: `function isVersionFlag(...)` → `export function isVersionFlag(...)`
No other changes to cli.ts (existing local call sites continue to work)
cli.ts is NOT on .claude/protected-paths.txt (verified) — no override note required
apps/oh build succeeds: `cd apps/oh && npm run build`
apps/oh typecheck passes: `cd apps/oh && npm run typecheck`
Smoke test: after build, `node apps/oh/dist/oh.js --help` exits 0 (verifies export change did not break tree-shaking or entry-point behavior)
userStories.notes: Completed 2026-05-24; commit dc83430. Added export to isHelpFlag (line 19) and isVersionFlag (line 23) in apps/oh/src/cli.ts. Typecheck and build pass; smoke test verifies --help exits 0.

userStories.title: Determinism and no-throw property for oh CLI flag detection
userStories.description: As a sandbox worker, I add a property test asserting isHelpFlag and isVersionFlag (now exported per US-007) are deterministic pure functions that never throw for any input.
acceptanceCriteria: New file apps/oh/src/__tests__/cli.property.test.ts exists
Imports `isHelpFlag`, `isVersionFlag` from `../cli.js` (NodeNext ESM extension per apps/oh/tsconfig.json)
Property tests using `fc.string()` and `fc.constant(undefined)` for the `string | undefined` signature:
- Determinism: `isHelpFlag(s) === isHelpFlag(s)` across repeated calls
- Determinism: `isVersionFlag(s) === isVersionFlag(s)` across repeated calls
- No-throw: neither function throws on any input
Mutual exclusion assertion explicitly NOT included (vacuously true for non-flag strings)
`npm test` from repo root passes, discovering the file via the extended vitest glob from US-001
Typecheck passes: `cd apps/oh && npm run typecheck`
userStories.notes: Completed 2026-05-24. Created apps/oh/src/__tests__/cli.property.test.ts with three property tests: isHelpFlag determinism, isVersionFlag determinism, and combined no-throw. Used vi.mock hoisting to stub process.exit before module import (cli.ts has a top-level main() call that fires process.exit). No fast-check added to apps/oh/package.json — root hoisting works. pnpm test passes: 121 tests, 12 files. Typecheck passes.

userStories.title: Document the property-testing convention
userStories.description: As a sandbox worker, I add docs/property-testing.md documenting the file-naming convention, arbitrary patterns, default numRuns, the per-surface ledger, and the decision tree for when to use property vs example tests. Link from root README.md for discoverability.
acceptanceCriteria: New file docs/property-testing.md exists
Section 'Convention' — file naming (*.property.test.ts), where tests live, what gets discovered by vitest
Section 'Arbitrary patterns' — fc.string, fc.array, fc.record, fc.stringMatching; when to write custom arbitraries (link to fast-check.dev)
Section 'numRuns' — default 100; when to tune
Section 'Decision tree' — one-paragraph: property test vs example test
Section 'Surface ledger' — markdown table listing 3 v1 surfaces (parseCronFile, path-guard, oh-cli-flags) with property names and which PR added each
Root README.md has a 'Testing' section (create if absent) with one-line link to docs/property-testing.md
CHANGELOG.md [Unreleased] entry references this work
Document does not duplicate fast-check upstream docs; links to fast-check.dev for reference
No code changes (docs-only story)
